description: Fix opentabs stop failing to stop servers started on a custom port, and fix the start command suggesting a stop command without --port

userStories.title: Store port in PID file so stop resolves the correct port
userStories.description: As a user who started the server with --port 8888, I want opentabs stop to stop the server on that port so that I don't end up with an orphaned server process I can't stop via CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: PID file at ~/.opentabs/server.pid stores both PID and port (e.g., JSON format {"pid":1234,"port":8888} or simple PID:PORT text format)

opentabs stop (without --port) reads the port from the PID file and uses it for the health-check verification at line 74 of stop.ts, instead of falling back to resolvePort(options) which defaults to 9515
When the PID file contains a port, that port is used for the health check even if --port is not passed and no config port is set
When --port is explicitly passed to stop, it overrides the PID file port (explicit flag takes precedence)
Backward compatibility: if the PID file contains only a plain integer (old format), stop falls back to resolvePort(options) as before
userStories.notes:
Root cause: platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts:73 calls resolvePort(options) to determine the health-check port, but resolvePort falls back to DEFAULT_PORT (9515) when no --port flag, no OPENTABS_PORT env, and no config.json port field exist. The PID file (written at platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts:383) only stores String(pid) — no port information.

The fix has two parts:
1. In start.ts:383, change the PID file write to include the port. Suggested format: JSON {"pid":1234,"port":8888} for extensibility, but a simple 'PID PORT' or 'PID\nPORT' text format also works. The key requirement is that the port is stored.
2. In stop.ts:49-90, after reading the PID file, extract the stored port and use it for the health check URL at line 74 (instead of calling resolvePort(options)). If the user passed --port explicitly, that should still win.

The PID file path comes from getPidFilePath() defined in platform/shared/src/constants.ts:52-53.

Important: the PID file write at start.ts:383 uses mode 0o600 — preserve this permission.

Important: backward compat — if the PID file contains only a number (old format from before this change), fall back to resolvePort(options). This handles the case where the user upgrades the CLI while a server is already running with an old-format PID file.

Do NOT change the PID file path or name — only the content format.

userStories.title: Include --port in the stop suggestion when started on custom port
userStories.description: As a user who started the server with --port 8888, I want the startup output to tell me the correct stop command (opentabs stop --port 8888) so that I know how to stop the server.
acceptanceCriteria: When opentabs start --background --port 8888 runs, the output line shows 'Stop: opentabs stop --port 8888' instead of 'Stop: opentabs stop'
When opentabs start --background runs on the default port (9515), the output still shows 'Stop: opentabs stop' (no --port flag needed)
userStories.notes:
The fix is in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts:394. Currently hardcoded as:
  console.log(pc.dim(`Stop: opentabs stop`));

Change to include --port when the resolved port differs from DEFAULT_PORT (9515, from @opentabs-dev/shared). The port value is available in the same scope — look for where 'port' is resolved earlier in the startBackground function (around line 344-357 where the child process is spawned with --port).

Import DEFAULT_PORT from @opentabs-dev/shared if not already imported. Check what's already imported at the top of the file.

Example output:
  Port 8888: 'Stop: opentabs stop --port 8888'
  Port 9515: 'Stop: opentabs stop'
